label: instance of
description: Asserts the entity is an instance (member) of a given class. Use this to say 'this program is a word processor' or 'this entity is a person'. The subject is a member, the object is the class.

label: subclass of
description: Asserts that all instances of the subject are also instances of the object. Use for class hierarchy: word-processor subclass_of documenta. Transitive — querying subclasses of @software returns all software classes.

label: member of
description: Asserts the entity belongs to a group, organization, consortium, or collection that is not a strict class-instance relationship. Use for org membership, standards bodies, or informal groupings.

label: Wikipedia article
description: The title slug of the English Wikipedia article for this entity (e.g. 'Microsoft_Word'). Used by check-links to verify article existence.

label: official URL
description: The canonical homepage or product URL for this entity. Prefer the most stable URL (not a version-specific page).

label: Wikidata ID
description: The Wikidata item ID (e.g. 'Q11268') for this entity. Enables cross-referencing with Wikidata during bulk ingest and link enrichment.

label: first released
description: ISO date (YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD) of the first public release or announcement of this entity. For programs, this is the first public version, not internal development start.

label: discontinued
description: ISO date when active development or distribution of this entity ceased. Omit if still active. Use rank=deprecated on later superseded_by statements to mark migration paths.

label: superseded by
description: The entity (usually a newer program or format) that officially replaces this one. Implies the subject is deprecated or end-of-life.

label: supersedes
description: The entity that this one officially replaces. Inverse of superseded_by.

label: start time
description: Used as a qualifier on statements to indicate when the asserted relationship began. ISO date. E.g. has_feature start_time=2013-04 for when a feature was added.

label: end time
description: Used as a qualifier on statements to indicate when the asserted relationship ended. ISO date. E.g. runs_on end_time=2020 for a platform that was dropped.

label: developed by
description: Organization or person primarily responsible for the development of this entity. Use for the team or company that owns and drives the project, not individual contributors.

label: develops
description: Inverse of developed_by. An organization or person develops a software entity.

label: principal author
description: Individual(s) who made the decisive intellectual or creative contribution to this entity. Distinct from developed_by (which points to organizations). Use qualifiers for role.

label: principal author of
description: Inverse of principal_author. A person is the principal author of a software entity.

label: current maintainer
description: Organization or person currently responsible for ongoing maintenance. May differ from developed_by if the project transferred stewardship.

label: licensed under
description: The license(s) under which this entity is distributed. Use @-prefixed entity ids for well-known licenses (e.g. @mit-license, @gpl-2).

label: written in
description: The programming language(s) in which this entity is primarily implemented. Use @-prefixed language entities.

label: runs on
description: Platforms or operating systems on which this entity can run. Use @-prefixed platform entities (e.g. @windows, @linux, @web).

label: implements
description: A specification, protocol, or standard that this entity conforms to or implements. Use for standards compliance claims.

label: compatible with
description: Another entity with which this entity interoperates. Weaker than implements — covers file format compatibility, API compatibility, or plugin compatibility.

label: depends on
description: A runtime or build-time dependency of this entity. Use for libraries, runtimes, or platforms required for the entity to function.

label: dependency of
description: Inverse of depends_on. This entity is a dependency of the subject.

label: version of
description: Asserts this entity is a specific version or release of a parent entity. Use for @ms-word-2007 version_of @ms-word.

label: has version
description: Inverse of version_of. A parent entity has a specific version entity.

label: descended from
description: The entity from which this one is directly derived, usually via a design lineage or spiritual inheritance (not necessarily a code fork). For direct code forks use forked_from. Transitive — chains through the lineage.

label: gave rise to
description: Inverse of descended_from. This entity directly gave rise to another.

label: forked from
description: The entity from which this one was directly forked (i.e. the codebase was copied and diverged). Implies shared ancestry at a specific point in time. More specific than descended_from.

label: has fork
description: Inverse of forked_from. This entity has a fork in the form of another entity.

label: influenced by
description: An entity that significantly shaped the design, features, or philosophy of this one without being a direct ancestor or fork. Use when the lineage is conceptual rather than genealogical. Transitive.

label: influenced
description: Inverse of influenced_by. This entity influenced another.

label: inspired by
description: An entity that provided creative or conceptual inspiration. Weaker and more diffuse than influenced_by — use when the designer cited it as an influence but the structural effect is unclear.

label: inspired
description: Inverse of inspired_by. This entity inspired another.

label: competes with
description: An entity occupying the same functional niche and market segment as this one. Symmetric in practice — if A competes with B, B competes with A. Include historical competitors even if one is discontinued.

label: replaces
description: This entity is the intended replacement or successor for the given entity in the same functional niche, from the same developer or organization. More specific than supersedes.

label: replaced by
description: Inverse of replaces. This entity was replaced by the given entity.

label: convergent with
description: An entity that independently evolved a similar feature or capability via a different lineage. Marks evolutionary convergence — use when two entities in different clades arrived at the same solution without shared ancestry.

label: has feature
description: A feature or capability this entity possesses. Use @-prefixed feature entities. Add qualifiers for start_time (when the feature was added) and end_time (if later removed).

label: vestigial feature
description: A feature this entity retains from an ancestor but which is no longer central to its function or identity. Analogous to biological vestigial organs. Add qualifiers for historical context.

label: acquired feature from
description: An entity from which this one adopted or ported a specific feature. Use in conjunction with has_feature to explain the feature's origin. Add a qualifier naming the feature entity.

label: synapomorphy
description: A shared derived characteristic that defines membership in a clade. String-valued (feature slug). Used on class entities to document the defining traits of that taxon.

label: etymology
description: The linguistic or conceptual origin of this entity's name. String-valued narrative.

label: rank hint
description: A biological-rank advisory for clade entities (e.g. 'kingdom', 'order'). Advisory only — the graph is rank-free. Distinct from statement-level rank (which is Wikidata statement priority).

label: type instance
description: The canonical or prototypical instance of this class entity. Analogous to a type species in biological taxonomy. Value is an @-prefixed entity id.
